events.title: Devastated Gaza will need over $70 billion to rebuild, international report warns
events.description: Gaza will need over seventy billion dollars in the next decade to rebuild from more than two years of devastating conflict. That's from a report this week from the UN, the EU and the World Bank, which has looked at the damage done after Israel went to war with Hamas in the Palestinian enclave following its attack on October 7th 2023.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Devastated Gaza will need over $70 billion to rebuild, international report warns

events.title: Canadian woman killed after gunman opens fire at Mexico’s Teotihuacán pyramids
events.description: At least four more injured at world heritage site in latest violent incident as country prepares to co-host World Cup One Canadian tourist has been killed and six other people were wounded by gunfire after an armed man opened fire at one of Mexico’s most famous tourist destinations, the Teotihuacán pyramids near Mexico City. The shooting – the latest violent incident to affect Mexico as it prepares to co-host the football World Cup in June – took place on Monday lunchtime and was captured in mobile phone videos. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Canadian woman killed after gunman opens fire at Mexico’s Teotihuacán pyramids

events.title: Will Iran war reshape global trade more than COVID?
events.description: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts wonder if this conflict will reshape supply chains more than the pandemic. Companies are already rethinking strategies to future-proof revenues from other geopolitical risks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Will Iran war reshape global trade more than COVID?

events.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
events.description: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds

events.title: Massive 7.7-magnitude earthquake hits off Japanese coast
events.description: A strong earthquake erupted off the coast of the nation of Japan, the Associated Press reported, noting the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center indicated the tsunami threat "has now passed."
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Massive 7.7-magnitude earthquake hits off Japanese coast

events.title: Who is Ahmad Vahidi? Iran’s new IRGC chief tied to global attacks and ‘Death to America’ ideology
events.description: Shadowy IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi is emerging as one of Iran's most powerful figures as Trump's ceasefire deadline approaches, analysts warn.

events.title: Millions of desperate Sudanese return home amid dire conditions as war rages
events.description: Three years into the devastating conflict in Sudan, nearly four million displaced people have returned to their places of origin across the country, only to face “another struggle for survival”, the UN International Organization for Migration (IOM) said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Millions of desperate Sudanese return home amid dire conditions as war rages

events.title: ‘We were terrified they were going to kill us’: fishers who survived US boat strike speak out
events.description: An Ecuadorian fishing crew describe their ordeal as victims of Trump’s purported war on ‘narcoterrorists’ By 4pm, the light was softening over the Pacific, and the crew of the Don Maca were finishing a long day hauling in lines of swordfish and albacore. Down in the hold, the mood had settled into the familiar rhythm of a fishing day drawing to a close. “We were just working, waiting for the last trawler to return,” said Jhonny Sebastián Palacios, one of the fishers. “Everything was perfectly fine.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘We were terrified they were going to kill us’: fishers who survived US boat strike speak out

events.title: Thirteen killed in second India fireworks blast in three days
events.description: Earlier this week, an explosion at a firecracker factory in neighbouring state Tamil Nadu left at least 25 dead.

events.title: Pope warns humanity's at risk, in new plea to world leaders
events.description: The Pope has warned humanity's future risks being "tragically compromised" due to a breakdown of international law and political responsibility.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope warns humanity's at risk, in new plea to world leaders

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Emergency briefing on Ukraine amid escalating Russian attacks
events.description: The Security Council meets in emergency session at 3pm to address the escalating conflict in Ukraine. The open briefing follows a formal request from Kyiv citing a surge in Russian aerial bombardments, including devastating strikes on the city of Dnipro. Ukraine has reported that between late March and mid-April, more than 5,000 drones and missiles were launched, killing dozens of civilians and injuring hundreds more. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users can follow here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Emergency briefing on Ukraine amid escalating Russian attacks

events.title: US military launches first autonomous warfare command to deploy unmanned systems across Latin America

events.title: Russia strikes Ukraine’s Odesa port, kills railway worker in Zaporizhia

events.title: 'We cannot let Iran be our spokesperson': Lebanon charts own path in talks with Israel
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Jad Shahrour, communications manager at the Samir Kassir Foundation. Lebanon is at a geopolitical crossroads, where sovereignty, regional rivalries and international diplomacy collide. Joining us from Beirut amid the ongoing uncertainty, Shahrour places the current negotiations with Israel within a broader struggle to reassert the authority of the Lebanese state. He emphasises the necessity of disentangling Lebanon’s national decision-making from external actors, while acknowledging the deeply rooted historical tensions that complicate any path toward stability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'We cannot let Iran be our spokesperson': Lebanon charts own path in talks with Israel

events.title: Five major issues affecting the FIFA World Cup with 50 days to go
events.description: From the US-Israel war on Iran to exorbitant ticket prices and violence in host nations, here's what's gone wrong.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Five major issues affecting the FIFA World Cup with 50 days to go

events.title: Trump extends ceasefire with Iran, says Tehran regime 'seriously fractured'
events.description: US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran to allow more time for talks, claiming the Islamic republic was "collapsing financially" because of the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. There was no word from Tehran on the ceasefire and an Iranian gunboat fired on a container ship off the coast of Oman, causing damage but no casualties, according to a British maritime security agency. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Philip Turle tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump extends ceasefire with Iran, says Tehran regime 'seriously fractured'

events.title: Moscow-born gunman dead after Kyiv shooting rampage leaves at least 6 dead, 14 wounded: Zelenskyy

events.title: Timor-Leste parliament questions president over proposed resort’s links to ‘scam’ empire
events.description: Jose Ramos-Horta urged by opposition to explain diplomatic passport given to businessman behind resort project, who denies any involvement with organised crime Timor-Leste’s opposition has questioned how foreign investors in a proposed cryptocurrency resort obtained prime beachfront real estate in the country’s capital, and has called on the president to explain why he issued a diplomatic passport to a Chinese businessman involved in the project. Speaking in parliament in Dili on Monday, Fretilin opposition party MP Florentino Ximenes da Costa “Sinarai” raised concerns about the proposed AB Digital Technology Resort, which was the subject of a months-long investigation by the Guardian and Organised Crime and Corruption Reporting Project (OCCRP) . Continue reading...

events.title: In south Lebanon village, Hezbollah buries fighters killed in war with Israel
events.description: Hezbollah held a mass funeral in south Lebanon's Kfar Sir on Tuesday for more than a dozen fighters killed during more than six weeks of war with Israel. The ceremony came after Israel and Lebanon entered into a 10-day ceasefire on Friday announced by US President Donald Trump. Iran-backed Hezbollah has not provided the total number of its fighters killed since Israel last month launched a massive wave of strikes and a ground invasion of Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 In south Lebanon village, Hezbollah buries fighters killed in war with Israel

events.title: Venezuela: 'Repressive apparatus remains in place,' says Amnesty International
events.description: In Venezuela, the state’s repressive apparatus “remains intact” after Nicolás Maduro’s overthrow, Amnesty International warns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Venezuela: 'Repressive apparatus remains in place,' says Amnesty International

events.title: World News in Brief: Myanmar amnesty, rising needs in Afghanistan, another power loss at Ukraine nuclear plant
events.description: Authorities in Myanmar released the country’s ousted president from prison on Friday, along with some 4,000 other people, as part of an amnesty to mark the traditional New Year festival.

events.title: Iran's workers face waves of layoffs as war batters domestic economy
events.description: Iranians are confronting mass layoffs and soaring inflation amid destroyed infrastructure and internet blackouts. We take a closer look. But first: US senators grill Donald Trump's nominee for Federal Reserve chair, and the global condom industry takes a blow from supply chain issues tied to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's workers face waves of layoffs as war batters domestic economy

events.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
events.description: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers. Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022, including a weekend that was El Salvador’s bloodiest since its civil war. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang

events.title: How grievance turns to violence in a mass shooter's mind
events.description: Attacks like last week's school shooting in Turkey can seem to come out of nowhere, but they rarely do. Instead, a buildup of grievance and fixation often precedes the the moment violent thoughts become violent acts.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 How grievance turns to violence in a mass shooter's mind

events.title: Iranian economy: Layoffs, inflation add to sanctions pain amid war
events.description: The ongoing conflict between the US and Israel has created a profound economic crisis in Iran. As the government deals with the crippling internet blackouts and soaring inflation, a sense of uncertainty has taken hold of the nation's workforce.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iranian economy: Layoffs, inflation add to sanctions pain amid war

events.title: Florida launches probe into OpenAI over ChatGPT’s alleged role in shooting
events.description: Florida has opened a criminal investigation into OpenAI following a deadly shooting in 2025 at Florida State University, Attorney General James ​Uthmeier said Tuesday. Authorities are looking into claims that the company's ChatGPT chatbot provided information to the suspect prior to the attack.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Florida launches probe into OpenAI over ChatGPT’s alleged role in shooting

It's unclear if more talks will take place in the coming day between Washington and Tehran in Islamabad. In Lebanon, a 10-day ceasefire with Israel continues to hold, with thousands returning to the south despite widespread destruction. A UN peacekeeper was also killed over the weekend – likely from Hezbollah fire. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.

events.title: Nature’s last strongholds: Why UNESCO sites are a lifeline for a planet in peril
events.description: In a world where the drumbeat of environmental loss is growing louder, a new landmark report from UNESCO has revealed a rare glimmer of hope. While global wildlife has plummeted by a staggering 73 per cent since the 1970s, life within the UN science and culture agency’s protected territories is holding its ground.

events.title: UN chief strongly condemns killing of another peacekeeper in southern Lebanon
events.description: A UN peacekeeper has been killed and three others injured after a UNIFIL patrol came under fire in southern Lebanon, the mission said on Saturday.

events.title: Student allegedly jailed in China for six years after taking part in pro-democracy protests in Australia
events.description: Exclusive: The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect overseas students from political repression The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect Chinese international students from political repression by authorities on their return after a Chinese student was allegedly sentenced to six years’ imprisonment for joining pro-democracy protests in Australia. The student, who the Guardian has chosen not to name, lost contact with his friends in Sydney after returning to China in December 2024. Continue reading...

events.title: Sam Neill says New Zealand goldmine supporters have threatened him with violence
events.description: Actor, who has publicly objected to plans to fast-track project near his farm, says he has received personal abuse The actor Sam Neill says he has received threats of violence from supporters of a controversial goldmine that could be opened several kilometres away from his farm in New Zealand’s Central Otago district, after he publicly objected to the government’s plans to fast-track the mine. The Australian mining company Santana Minerals is pushing to expedite a 85-hectare (210-acre) open-cast goldmine, called Bendigo-Ophir, in the Dunstan mountains, an area described as “outstanding natural landscape” by the Central Otago district council. Continue reading...

events.title: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex
events.description: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said. Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers with lower stockpiles than usual, CEO Goh Miah Kiat told Reuters in an interview on Tuesday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex

events.title: Gas lobby spends millions on anti-tax ads – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Gas companies spending millions on Australian advertising blitz to fight export tax, inquiry told Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Farmers promised more fertiliser imports Australian farmers are being promised more fertiliser imports under a deal struck between the federal government and two major companies, aimed at securing supplies in response to a global bottleneck, AAP reports. We’ve always said through this generational reform process that we’d listen to older people and we’d respond to their experiences. … What they’ve made clear is that they want showering and dressing. We’ve got a $40bn aged care system … and it needs to be sustainable for generations to come. And that’s what this reform process has been about. … We can’t be in a situation where we’re making a promise to the people of Australia about the dignity that they’ll receive through the aged care system if we can’t deliver on it and we can’t keep it sustainable. Continue reading...

events.title: Two US officials who died after Mexico drug raid reported to be CIA agents
events.description: Mexico to investigate possible breach of its constitution and assess US’s role in anti-drug operation near Chihuahua Mexico has launched an investigation into a possible breach of its constitution as it was reported that two US embassy officials who died in a car accident while returning from a raid on a drug lab with local officials in the border state of Chihuahua were CIA operatives. The accident happened early on Sunday, as the officials were driving back from the scene of the raid. Their vehicle skidded off the road and plunged down a 200 metre ravine in the mountains near Chihuahua’s border with the state of Sinaloa. Continue reading...

events.title: IRGC asserting themselves as Iran 'responds to Trump’s method of conducting diplomacy in kind'
events.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Dr Burcu Ozcelik, Senior Research Fellow for Middle East Security within the Int'l Security Dept at RUSI. Dr. Ozcelik argues that Iran is simultaneously responding to external pressures: particularly the performative diplomacy of the Trump administration while safeguarding core sovereign priorities, including its nuclear program and regional influence. What may appear as inconsistency is, in fact, a dual strategy: rhetorical escalation paired with cautious pragmatism. Iran and the US are navigating mounting economic and geopolitical constraints.

events.title: Ukraine to restart oil flows via Druzhba pipeline, potentially unblocking EU's loan
events.description: Ukraine ​will resume pumping oil through the Druzhba pipeline on Wednesday, an industry source said, after President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said repairs were complete and urged the EU to unblock a 90-billion-euro loan halted by an argument over the pipe. A lengthy suspension of oil flows through the pipeline following a Russian attack on a pumping facility in Ukraine in January ​sparked an angry backlash ‌from European Union members Hungary and Slovakia, which remain reliant on Russian oil imports via Druzhba.

events.title: Peru's electoral commission chief resigns over chaotic vote
events.description: The head of Peru's electoral body resigned from his post Tuesday, hours before he was scheduled to be questioned by prosecutors over the chaotic presidential vote earlier this month. Peruvians went to the polls on April 12 to choose their ninth president in a decade, with the election dominated by voter anger at a political establishment seen as corrupt. The election day was marred by major logistical problems in the capital Lima, which prevented tens of thousands of people from voting until the following day.

events.title: Displaced Palestinians return to books as Gaza's Phoenix library rises from the rubble
events.description: Displaced Palestinians browse books at Gaza's new Phoenix Library, a youth-led initiative in the Rimal neighbourhood that aims to revive culture and literature during the war. The library houses a diverse collection of literary and scientific books, and includes a children's section, its name reflecting Gaza's rise from the rubble. "We retrieved the books from amidst the rubble many of which were in poor condition and proceeded to sort and clean them," says library owner Omar Hamad.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Displaced Palestinians return to books as Gaza's Phoenix library rises from the rubble

events.title: Charlize Theron joins chorus of disapproval over Timothée Chalamet’s ballet comments

In an interview with the New York Times , Theron said: “Oh, boy, I hope I run into him one day,” adding: “That was a very reckless comment on two art forms that we need to lift up constantly because, yes, they do have a hard time. But in 10 years, AI is going to be able to do Timothée’s job, but it will not be able to replace a person on a stage dancing live.” Continue reading...

events.title: Gas companies spending millions on Australian advertising blitz to fight export tax, inquiry told
events.description: Shell Australia says campaign needed to ‘counterbalance’ claims by levy advocates – but Ed Husic urges industry ‘do not spend millions defending the indefensible’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Gas companies are mounting a multimillion-dollar advertising campaign to fight against a new export tax, prompting the Labor MP Ed Husic to accuse the industry of “defending the indefensible”. Shell Australia is among half a dozen oil and gas companies contributing around $1m to an Australian Energy Producers (AEP) campaign that attempts to justify the amount of tax the industry pays, a parliamentary inquiry heard on Wednesday. Continue reading...

events.title: M 7.4 - 100 km ENE of Miyako, Japan
events.description: PAGER - GREEN ShakeMap - VI DYFI? - VI Time 2026-04-20 07:53:00 UTC 2026-04-20 07:53:00 UTC at epicenter Location 39.953°N 143.046°E Depth 35.00 km (21.75 mi)

events.title: Priceless 2,500-year-old golden helmet returned to Romania after Dutch museum raid
events.description: The ornate Cotofenesti helmet, which was stolen in January 2025 while on loan to a Dutch museum, was recovered last month.

events.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
events.description: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

events.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
events.description: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

events.title: Germany news: Lufthansa scraps 20,000 flights
events.description: As Frankfurt Airport opens a new terminal, Lufthansa has scrapped 20,000 flights due to soaring fuel prices. Meanwhile, Defense Minister Pistorius has unveiled his future strategy for Germany's armed forces. DW has more.

events.title: China hails countries for blocking Taiwan president's flight
events.description: Taiwan's president canceled a planned trip to Eswatini after three African countries revoked permission for his plane to fly through their airspace. Beijing has denied pressuring the countries to block the flight.
events.ticker_label: 💥 China hails countries for blocking Taiwan president's flight

events.title: Record number of immigrants reside in EU in 2025
events.description: The foreign‑born population of the EU grew to around 64 million in 2025. Germany remains the biggest host of immigrants in absolute terms, but some smaller nations have a much higher percentage of foreign-born residents.

events.title: Digital memory at stake: Why news outlets block the Wayback Machine
events.description: The "Wayback Machine," custodian of digital memory, is fighting for its survival. An increasing number of media outlets are refusing to allow the Web Archive to archive their content.

events.title: Australia targets Roblox, Minecraft,  Fortnite and Steam in child safety push
events.description: Roblox, Minecraft, Fortnite and Steam were among the gaming services asked by authorities to explain how they are protecting children from sexual predators and radicalization.

events.title: Virginia voters approve plan that could boost Democrats' seats in Congress
events.description: The move could increase the Democrats' chances of winning four currently Republican-held seats in Congress in midterm elections in November.

events.title: Nigeria charges six over alleged Tinubu overthrow plot
events.description: The alleged plot comes after 16 military officers stood trial in January over a coup attempt against president Bola Tinubu.

events.title: Zelenskyy says Druzhba oil pipeline to Europe repaired
events.description: A multibillion euro aid package from the EU had been blocked over access to the pipeline, which brings Russian oil to countries like Hungary and Slovakia.

events.title: Victor Wembanyama sustains concussion in Spurs' Game 2 loss
events.description: San ⁠Antonio ⁠star center Victor Wembanyama ⁠was concussed during ​Game 2 of the Spurs' first-round playoff series with the Portland Trail Blazers ​on Tuesday, his coach confirmed following the contest. Wembanyama hit his face on the floor at near-full speed after being knocked off his feet during a drive to the ‌basket at the 8:57 mark of the second period, with the contact leaving him dazed enough to struggle ​standing up before jogging off the floor to the locker room.

events.title: NASA says new space telescope will create 'atlas of the universe'
events.description: NASA on Tuesday unveiled the Nancy Grace Roman space telescope, a powerful new observatory designed to map the universe and search for distant planets. Aiming to shed light on dark matter and dark energy, the new telescope will be able to observe light emitted by celestial bodies billions of years ago thanks to its infrared vision.

events.title: Mogul behind K-pop supergroup faces arrest
events.description: South Korean police are seeking to arrest the music mogul behind K-pop supergroup BTS over an alleged £100m investor fraud scheme.

events.title: Two IDF soldiers jailed over smashing of Jesus statue
events.description: Two Israeli soldiers have been jailed for 30 days and removed from combat duty after a statue of Jesus was destroyed.

events.title: Hundreds of gang members in mass trial over more than 47,000 crimes
events.description: Hundreds of MS-13 gang members accused of homicides, drug and arms trafficking, and disappearances are the subject of a mass trial in El Salvador.

events.title: Airline cuts 20,000 flights
events.description: Lufthansa has said 20,000 short-haul flights are being cut from its schedule this summer, allowing it to save on the soaring cost of jet fuel.

events.title: IDF soldier damages Jesus statue with sledgehammer in Lebanon - as Netanyahu responds
events.description: Israel's prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu condemned "in the strongest terms" images of an Israeli soldier damaging a statue of Jesus.

events.title: Ukraine wants a Zelenskyy-Putin summit to jolt stalled US-led peace efforts
events.description: Kyiv’s top diplomat says Ukraine is pushing for direct talks between President Zelenskyy and Russian President Putin

events.title: Pope calls for justice and closing income gap in Equatorial Guinea Mass with presidential family
events.description: Pope Leo XIV is urging Equatorial Guineans to work for freedom, justice and to close the gap “between the privileged and the disadvantaged.”

events.title: How 2 men claimed an absurd record by driving an old 3-wheel car the length of Africa
events.description: Two friends have claimed a bizarre record by driving a decades-old British three-wheel car about 14,000 miles from the U.K. to the southern tip of Africa for the longest trip by a three-wheel vehicle

events.title: Nearly 500 alleged MS-13 members are on a mass trial in El Salvador
events.description: Nearly 500 alleged gang leaders are on a collective, mass trial in El Salvador, a practice criticized by human rights groups

events.title: Priceless 2,500-year-old golden helmet returned to Romania after Dutch museum raid
events.description: A 2,500-year-old golden helmet stolen last year from a Dutch museum where it was on loan has been returned to Romania

events.title: Ukraine completes Druzhba pipeline repairs, hoping to unlock blocked EU loan
events.description: Ukraine has completed repairs on a damaged oil pipeline and is preparing to resume flows

events.title: NATO intercepts Russian military aircraft flying over the Baltic Sea
events.description: NATO has intercepted Russian bombers and fighter jets over the Baltic Sea
events.ticker_label: 💥 NATO intercepts Russian military aircraft flying over the Baltic Sea

events.title: The head of Myanmar's army-backed government proposes new peace talks with armed resistance groups
events.description: The head of Myanmar's military-backed government has invited armed resistance groups to new peace talks

events.title: Economic collapse pushes highly educated Gazans into the ‘survival economy’
events.description: Young Palestinians in Gaza with university-level educations are setting aside dreams of putting their hard-won skills into practice and doing whatever they can to survive.

events.title: Secretary-General hopefuls make their case in televised ‘interactive dialogues’
events.description: The four candidates so far in the running to be the next UN Secretary-General will each have the chance to show why they are the best choice during a series of “interactive dialogues” taking place at UN Headquarters in New York this week.

events.title: Gates Foundation plans to cut up to 500 jobs while undergoing review of Jeffrey Epstein ties
events.description: The Gates Foundation plans to cut up to 500 jobs by 2030 as part of a long-term restructuring while an external review of its Epstein ties is underway.

events.title: Doctor indicted on 81 new sexual assault charges in case involving dozens of victims
events.description: Massachusetts doctor Derrick Todd is indicted on 81 new charges including rape and assault allegedly involving dozens of his hospital patients.

events.title: High school counselor pleads guilty to multiple sex crimes involving teen she was meant to help
events.description: North Carolina high school counselor and volleyball coach sentenced to up to 40 years in prison after admitting to sexually abusing a teenage student.

events.title: Homeowners in leafy enclaves hit by burglars zeroing in on high-end homes for smash-and-grab scores
events.description: A wave of residential burglaries across Los Angeles County prompts Mayor Karen Bass to direct LAPD to deploy additional patrols along Ventura Boulevard.

events.title: COVID scientist accused of using ChatGPT in alleged poisoning plot against rival
events.description: A UW-Madison scientist allegedly admitted to poisoning a colleague's water bottle with toxic chemicals after feeling slighted by a promotion, court documents say.
